<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- All entrypoints depend on `config.js`, keeping behavior centralized.
- Help/test coverage is currently focused on CLI flows, leaving ci/web entrypoints lighter.
- Entry files route through a single `config.js`, so extend that module with feature-flag toggles instead of scattering env reads to preserve the current zero-coupling boundary.
- `help.js` drives the shared UX but only `tests/unit/help.test.js` exercises it; add ci/web-specific cases to lock down argument parity across surfaces.
- Zone cohesion is perfect, so maintain that by keeping any new adapters (e.g., HTTP listeners) thin wrappers that call into existing entrypoint exports rather than importing from other zones.
- Call graph data (284 internal calls, zero external edges) shows this zone operates as a sealed façade over CLI/CI/Web entrypoints, so any future integration must flow through its existing config surface instead of direct cross-zone imports.
- Façade pattern: the entrypoints form a self-contained shell with no cross-zone edges, keeping user-facing surfaces isolated from the agent/tooling stack.
